scenario_title: Add_rr to a name that already owns a CNAME
test_rationale: The zone starts with c.campus.edu. owning only a CNAME, then performs a single UPDATE that adds an A RR at the same owner with no prerequisites. This directly exercises the undefined interaction between RFC 2136 dynamic add semantics and RFC 2181 CNAME exclusivity. The two follow-up queries independently observe whether the CNAME survived and whether the A rrset was created, making both the UPDATE response and the resulting zone state suitable for a binary oracle.

group_1.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE because the owner already has a CNAME and does not permit adding other data there.
group_1.post_state: QUERY returns only the original CNAME at c.campus.edu. and no A rrset.

group_2.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE and normalizes the node by replacing or removing the CNAME so the A can exist.
group_2.post_state: QUERY returns an A rrset at c.campus.edu., with the CNAME absent.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 says a CNAME owner should not have other data, but RFC 2136 does not specify how an UPDATE that adds a non-CNAME RR to such a name must be processed. Implementations may reject the update, or may accept it by implicitly removing the CNAME or otherwise changing post-state, yielding divergent reply and query-visible results.
